prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_rf', seasonality_champion='DWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.859) and high seasonality variance (0.6889), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_rf leads trend tracking while DWT_catboost excels at seasonality. The true series shows mixed trends (down then up). Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal to leverage diverse model perspectives without over-weighting any single approach.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "FT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "ONLY_FT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_svr", "DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "DWT_catboost", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ARIMA", "rf", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_rf", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.859,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.859) and high seasonality variance (0.6889), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_rf leads trend tracking while DWT_catboost excels at seasonality. The true series shows mixed trends (down then up). Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal to leverage diverse model perspectives without over-weighting any single approach."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "robust_trimmed_mean_0.20"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates diverse per-horizon best models, favoring topk selection over full mean. relative_spread_mean=0.768 (≥0.25) signals high disagreement requiring robust methods like dba_combination and trimmed_mean. RMSE spread ratio=1.859 with FT_svr(20190.95) vs NaiveMovingAverage(6501.59) extreme gap justifies inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.6889). n_windows=3 requires small top_k and higher shrinkage to avoid overfitting."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity (1 baseline=dba_combination, 1 weighted=inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35, 1 selection=topk_mean_per_horizon_k3). All candidates are safe from leakage (dba_combination is a combination method, inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage shrinkage, topk_mean uses per-horizon selection). Score margin between top2 candidates is only 0.003 indicating well-balanced performance. Universe leaderboards show ade_dynamic_error candidates with lower RMSE but also lower overall scores (0.836 vs 0.88+), suggesting higher variance. With n_windows=3, current weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 is sufficient. No redundancy issues (only 1 of each type).",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0", "exp_weighted_average_eta0.5_trim1.0", "trimmed_mean_r0.20"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has only 3 candidates with high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.768≥0.25). Universe shows ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0 (RMSE=5845.92) and exp_weighted_average_eta0.5_trim1.0 (RMSE=5839.18) beat current best (RMSE=6074.69). n_windows=3 is low, so adding diverse weighted methods helps. Adding trimmed_mean_r0.20 provides robustness against outliers. No removals needed - current candidates are performing well.", "changes": ["added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard", "added exp_weighted_average_eta0.5_trim1.0 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard", "added trimmed_mean_r0.20 for outlier robustness with low n_windows"], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.859) and high seasonality variance (0.6889), indicating models capture patterns very differently. DWT_rf leads trend tracking while DWT_catboost excels at seasonality. The true series shows mixed trends (down then up). Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal to leverage diverse model perspectives without over-weighting any single approach.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates diverse per-horizon best models, favoring topk selection over full mean. relative_spread_mean=0.768 (≥0.25) signals high disagreement requiring robust methods like dba_combination and trimmed_mean. RMSE spread ratio=1.859 with FT_svr(20190.95) vs NaiveMovingAverage(6501.59) extreme gap justifies inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.6889). n_windows=3 requires small top_k and higher shrinkage to avoid overfitting.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity (1 baseline=dba_combination, 1 weighted=inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35, 1 selection=topk_mean_per_horizon_k3). All candidates are safe from leakage (dba_combination is a combination method, inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage shrinkage, topk_mean uses per-horizon selection). Score margin between top2 candidates is only 0.003 indicating well-balanced performance. Universe leaderboards show ade_dynamic_error candidates with lower RMSE but also lower overall scores (0.836 vs 0.88+), suggesting higher variance. With n_windows=3, current weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 is sufficient. No redundancy issues (only 1 of each type).

statistician.rationale: Current set has only 3 candidates with high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.768≥0.25). Universe shows ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0 (RMSE=5845.92) and exp_weighted_average_eta0.5_trim1.0 (RMSE=5839.18) beat current best (RMSE=6074.69). n_windows=3 is low, so adding diverse weighted methods helps. Adding trimmed_mean_r0.20 provides robustness against outliers. No removals needed - current candidates are performing well.
changes: added ade_dynamic_error_beta0.3_trim1.0 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard
added exp_weighted_average_eta0.5_trim1.0 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard
added trimmed_mean_r0.20 for outlier robustness with low n_windows
